title: Cookies, Credential Modes, API Keys, Bearer Tokens, and Secret Hygiene · Automation and Data Client Systems · Python Build Room
summary: A civic-data automation team integrating a staging catalog must build a first protocol model from narrated fixtures and explain each policy boundary for cookies, credential modes, api keys, bearer tokens, and secret hygiene, producing least-authority credential and session policy. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers least-authority credential and session policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through pr…
objectives: Apply cookie domain, host-only, path, expiry, Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite scope through a deliberate jar policy. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Recognize ambient cookie authority and require CSRF defenses for state-changing browser requests. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Place API keys in approved headers or signing inputs and exclude them from URLs, source, telemetry, and error text. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

steps.title: Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Cookie Jar Scope
steps.instruction:
workshop case — civic-data staging importer handles host-only session cookie SID-4, SameSite policy, API key, and expiring bearer token while two accounts run concurrently. Define jar isolation, CSRF, scope, rotation, redaction, and redirect boundaries.

What must be established before implementation?

At Python Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop Credential Provider Design, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: civic-data staging importer handles host-only session cookie SID-2, SameSite policy, API key, and expiring bearer token while two accounts run concurrently. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Inject short-lived credentials through a testable provider with rotation, concurrency, redaction, and failure policy. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
